Identification.LongTitle: An Act respecting Christian Heritage Month
Identification.RunningHead: An Act respecting Christian Heritage Month

Provision.Text: This enactment designates the month of December as âChristian Heritage Monthâ.

Whereas, in the conferences and negotiations that brought about Confederation, Canada was originally named âDominion of Canadaâ, a name reportedly inspired by the passage in the Bible (King James Version) at Psalm 72:8, which states: âHe shall have dominion also from sea to sea, and from the river unto the ends of the earthâ;
Whereas the month of December marks significant events and celebrations in the Christian calendar, from the beginning of Advent to the celebration of the birth of Jesus Christ;
And whereas, according to Statistics Canadaâs 2021 Census of Population, Christianity is the largest religion in Canada, with over half of Canadians identifying as Christian;
Now, therefore, His Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the Christian Heritage Month Act.
Throughout Canada, in each year, the month of December is to be known as âChristian Heritage Monthâ.
